behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the heuristic algorithm itself (MOCK-MISS: single function/object logic). No real OS/network/subprocess needed (REAL-INFRA: no). No AI dependency (LLM-DEP: no). Integration was considered and rejected because the behavior text is strictly about route-quality math, not module handoff side effects.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic directly on representative coordinate sets (including adversarial layouts), compute optimal route length with an exact solver for tractable sizes, and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: Real failure commonly appears at module handoff: leg-distance output + road-type speed config lookup + estimator formula (MOCK-MISS: yes, cross-module). No real OS/network/subprocess required (REAL-INFRA: no). No AI dependency (LLM-DEP: no). System rejected because behavior does not require real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real distance/leg model, road-type speed configuration, and time-estimation service in-process; feed a multi-leg route with mixed road types and assert per-leg and total time windows match expected calculations.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure validation arithmetic and threshold logic on provided totals (MOCK-MISS: no module interaction required). No real infra needed (REAL-INFRA: no). No AI dependency (LLM-DEP: no). Integration rejected because behavior text isolates validator responsibility.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke validator with edge-case totals (equal, just over, far over, fractional hours) and assert rejection boolean plus exact excess-time value.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Failure is orchestration across geocoding client result handling, filtering, optimizer input, and skip reporting (MOCK-MISS: yes, multi-module handoff). Real HTTP is not required to catch this specific failure; an in-process geocoder stub returning 'no results' is sufficient (REAL-INFRA: no, so not System). No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with real orchestration modules and a stub geocoding adapter that returns no results for one input; assert that address is reported as skipped, excluded from optimizer input, and final route is built from remaining stops.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Needs deduplication + item-merge + routing handoff verification ('not visited twice') across modules (MOCK-MISS: yes). No real OS/network/subprocess required (REAL-INFRA: no). No AI dependency (LLM-DEP: no). Unit rejected because checking route visitation requires at least dedupe-to-optimizer integration.
behaviors.test_description: Pass input containing duplicate addresses with different delivery items through in-process service pipeline; assert one consolidated stop with merged items is sent to routing and appears exactly once in produced route.
